input.initialPrompt: Analyze this project for incremental build issues. Identify any custom targets that would unnecessarily re-run on every build even when nothing has changed.

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to analyze the project for incremental build issues. Let me look at the project files to understand what's there.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>
25.

     </PropertyGroup>

     </Exec>

                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
                      Overwrite="true" />
     <ItemGroup>

     </ItemGroup>
   </Target>
 </Project>

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

Both custom targets will **re-run on every build** even when nothing has changed, because they lack `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6)
- **Problem:** No `Inputs`/`Outputs` specified, so MSBuild can never skip it. Worse, it writes `$([System.DateTime]::Now)` — a volatile value that changes every build — which then touches the timestamp of `build-timestamp.txt`, potentially cascading into further rebuilds.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13)
- **Problem:** No `Inputs`/`Outputs` specified, so it always re-runs. It executes `git rev-parse HEAD` and writes `git-hash.g.cs` unconditionally. Even if the git hash hasn't changed, the file is overwritten (new timestamp), which forces Roslyn to recompile.

### Fixes

| Target | Fix |
|--------|-----|
| `GenerateTimestamp` | Either remove it (timestamps defeat incrementality by definition) or add `Inputs`/`Outputs` and only write when content differs. |
| `EmbedGitHash` | Add `Inputs="$(ProjectDir).git/HEAD;$(ProjectDir).git/refs/heads/**"` and `Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"`. Also use a content-comparison write (write to a `.tmp`, then copy only if different) to avoid touching the file when the hash hasn't changed. |

The root cause in both cases: **missing `Inputs`/`Outputs` declarations** mean MSBuild has no way to determine the target is up-to-date, so it rebuilds every time.
